frozen_components: NeuroLogic Decoding framework (pruning, grouping, selecting logic)
Moses toolkit word alignment and translation table assembly
architecture.summary: The system extends NeuroLogic Decoding to perform edit-constrained decoding for sentence simplification. It expands beam search with three types of edit-based constraints (insertion, deletion, substitution) that replicate simplification operations. Each constraint type has strict satisfaction conditions evaluated during beam search: insertion rewards output tokens matching constraint targets; deletion requires the unwanted token to appear as a sibling node in the beam lattice; substitution requires both source and target tokens to appear as siblings. Constraint satisfaction is scored and accumulated across timesteps, biasing beam search toward hypotheses that satisfy more edit operations with higher scores.
trained_components: BART-base sequence-to-sequence model (fine-tuned on simplification corpora)
BERT-base constraint prediction model (fine-tuned for token classification of edit operations)

claims.statement: The proposed method largely outperforms previous studies using lexically constrained decoding on oracle constraints.
The proposed method achieved significantly higher constraint satisfaction rates compared to Zetsu et al. (2022) on oracle constraints.
The stricter satisfaction conditions for deletion and substitution operations avoid sub-optimal generation that satisfies only easy constraints at early timesteps.

Manual error analysis on ASSET shows the proposed method decreased errors in failed named entity generation, dropped information in complex sentences, and altered meaning/focus compared to Zetsu et al. (2022).

baseline.name: Zetsu et al. (2022), Agrawal et al. (2021), Kajiwara (2019)

claims.statement: With predicted constraints, the proposed method consistently outperformed baselines on SARI across all three corpora, while previous methods (Agrawal et al. 2021, Kajiwara 2019) showed instability.
Constraint prediction precision on substitution operations was low, with room for improvement particularly in deletion and substitution operations.
The constraint weights are currently constant and applied from the beginning of generation, which often excessively affects generation and ends up in degenerate solutions.
The method was limited to BART-base and did not explore application to large language models or models trained on larger corpora, leaving full potential unrealized.
